stemHtml: A flight is being planned with poor weather conditions forecast en route. The fuel calculation has been completed according to standard fuel policy requirements. The average fuel consumption rate is 4000 kg/hr. After reviewing the fuel plan, the Captain decides to add an additional 4000 kg of fuel to the planned fuel load due to the uncertain weather conditions. What fuel classification applies to this supplementary 4000 kg?

options.a: Discretionary fuel - because it was the Captain's decision to add extra fuel beyond regulatory requirements.
options.b: Final reserve fuel (FINRES) - because it provides an additional hour of holding capability.
options.c: Trip fuel - because it may be consumed during the en-route phase due to weather deviations.
options.d: Additional fuel - because it is required for contingencies beyond the standard fuel planning requirements.
